strategic_profile.executive_summary: Founded in 1839 as the first public university west of the Mississippi, Mizzou is an R1 land-grant institution and AAU member known for the 'Missouri Method' of experiential learning [cite: 1, 3]. It hosts the world's first journalism school and provides over 300 academic degree programs [cite: 1, 9].

analyst_takeaways.insight: General admission is highly accessible (78.5% acceptance), but upper-division progression into Journalism and Business is highly competitive and gated by strict GPA prerequisites [cite: 4, 7, 8].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should easily gain entry to the university but must be prepared for rigorous 'weeder' coursework to declare their final major.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The 'Missouri Method' guarantees real-world, hands-on learning experiences, especially in media, healthcare, and engineering [cite: 3].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Graduates possess highly tangible resume experience, directly contributing to the university's 95.4% successful outcome rate [cite: 21].

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus environment is heavily dominated by SEC sports traditions, tailgating, and a massive Greek life presence [cite: 2].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Ideal for students seeking a spirited, large-scale traditional college experience, though potentially overwhelming for those looking for a quiet academic enclave.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and traditional SEC college town atmosphere with over 600 student organizations, intense school spirit, and a prominent Greek system [cite: 2].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located centrally in Columbia, MO; functions as a quintessential, self-contained college town with highly walkable campus resources [cite: 4].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: R1 Doctoral University with 'Very High Research Activity' reporting $462 million in annual research and development expenditures [cite: 1].

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional pilot through Fall 2027. Test-optional applicants undergo holistic review and must submit a resume and short essay. Homeschool applicants cannot apply test-optional [cite: 5, 6, 11].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Mizzou admits on a rolling basis until the class is full; historical data shows no undergraduate waitlist is utilized [cite: 4, 7].

gpa.notes: Average high school GPA of matriculating freshmen is 3.6 [cite: 1].

testing.policy_note: 66% of applicants submit ACT scores, and 10% submit SAT scores [cite: 4, 7, 12].

colleges.name: Trulaske College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking structured professional development and strong placement into 'Big Four' accounting firms [cite: 13].

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Requires 2.8 GPA for students with under 30 credits, or a 3.0 GPA for students with 30+ credits to officially declare the major [cite: 8].
majors.notes: Students must complete the Trulaske Professional EDGE program, which mandates internships and professional seminars [cite: 13].

colleges.name: Missouri School of Journalism
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for aspiring journalists, broadcasters, and media professionals willing to do hands-on field reporting immediately [cite: 4].

majors.name: Journalism
majors.special_requirements: Students initially enter in pre-admission status and must maintain rigorous core and journalism GPAs to progress to upper-level newsrooms [cite: 4, 14].
majors.notes: Features the 'Missouri Method' allowing students to work in actual NBC, NPR, and print media affiliates [cite: 3].

application_deadlines.notes: December 1 is the priority deadline for all merit-based scholarships. Applications open August 1 [cite: 6, 22].

supplemental_requirements.details: Students applying without test scores must submit a resume and answer one short essay question for holistic review [cite: 5].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Mizzou relies heavily on automatic academic thresholds for standard admission, reserving holistic review primarily for borderline candidates or test-optional applicants [cite: 4, 11].

major_selection_tactics: Apply to 'Exploring/Undeclared' within the Discovery Center if unsure to safely fulfill prerequisites [cite: 24].
Test-optional applicants must ensure their core GPA recalculation is exceptionally high to compensate [cite: 11].
Apply before December 1 to maximize automatic merit aid, especially as an out-of-state applicant [cite: 6].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Entering as an 'Exploring' student does not heavily penalize applicants, as most professional schools (like Business and Journalism) require pre-major status and internal prerequisite completion anyway [cite: 4, 24].

financials.tuition_model: Flat-rate tuition model charging a single price for 12-18 credit hours per semester [cite: 15].
financials.aid_philosophy: Need-Blind, but does not guarantee meeting 100% of demonstrated need. Offers heavy merit-based discounting [cite: 15, 19].

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholars
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition, room, board, and a stipend. Highly competitive, historically requiring 32 ACT / 1420 SAT [cite: 18].

scholarships.name: Mark Twain Nonresident Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Substantial tuition reduction for out-of-state students based on academics and test scores [cite: 19].

scholarships.name: George C. Brooks Scholar Award
scholarships.benefits: Grants in-state tuition rate plus $7,500 per year to underrepresented minority students [cite: 25].

ap_policy.general_rule: AP credit is generally accepted for students meeting the required minimum score for specific exams [cite: 26].
ap_policy.usage: Credits are applied toward general education and major prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit granted for Higher Level (HL) examinations only. No credit granted for Subsidiary Level (SL) [cite: 27].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: CLEP credit only awarded to students possessing fewer than 90 hours of college credit [cite: 27].

what_it_takes: Rigorous completion of the 17-unit high school core curriculum [cite: 11].
Applying early (before Dec 1) to secure a spot and scholarships [cite: 6].
A strong transcript and resume for test-optional candidates [cite: 5].
essay_tips: For test-optional applicants, clearly articulate your strengths and extenuating circumstances [cite: 11].
red_flags: Low core GPA without test scores to compensate [cite: 11].
Applying after December 1 if reliant on merit scholarships [cite: 6].
